notes: Seed crystal for the Invariant Ontology (CFS-002). status=proposed until ratified via canonization; ratified_source marks statements drawn verbatim from ratified Polity documents.

Constitutional memory is append-only; supersession replaces deletion.
Identifiers that re-identify a subject never leave the server.

invariants.statement: Preserve before replace.

invariants.statement: The Polity defines why; AgentiQ defines how; Chrysalis defines how it evolves.

invariants.statement: Invariants themselves accrue Standing.

provenance.source: CFS-001 §6.2 Invariant Standing

invariants.statement: Truth is established through validation within a domain of applicability, not by popularity.

provenance.source: CFS-009 Law XII — Truth, Standing and Reach

invariants.statement: Standing expresses constitutional confidence in an invariant and shall never be interpreted as a measure of truth.

provenance.source: CFS-009 Law XII — Truth, Standing and Reach

invariants.statement: Reach measures adoption rather than validity; constitutional knowledge preserves the distinction between Truth, Standing, and Reach.

provenance.source: CFS-009 Law XII — Truth, Standing and Reach

invariants.statement: Personhood precedes individualization. Individualization precedes standing. Identity is an optional derivative of individualization rather than a prerequisite for constitutional participation.

provenance.source: CFS-009 Law XIII — Individualization

invariants.statement: Individualization is the establishment of a constitutional subject capable of accruing Standing without that subject being identifiable.

provenance.source: CFS-009 Law XIII — Individualization

invariants.statement: Individualization is defined by continuity, not disclosure — a constitutional subject's persistence across time and interaction is what allows validated action to accumulate into Standing, independent of whether that subject is ever identified.

provenance.source: CFS-009 Law XIII, Corollary I

invariants.statement: Identity is a branch of individualization, not its gate; it yields reputation, not standing.

provenance.source: CFS-009 Law XIII, Corollary II

invariants.statement: Individualization's continuity shall be maintained without requiring re-identification by any party, including the server — a target which current server-internal (T0) implementations approximate but do not define the limit of.

provenance.source: CFS-009 Law XIII, Corollary III

invariants.statement: The protagonist's appearance, wardrobe, and age remain identical across every segment of a production.

provenance.source: CFS-011 §3.1 Visual Identity

invariants.statement: Lighting language, colour palette, and camera language remain identical across every segment of a production.

provenance.source: CFS-011 §3.1 Visual Identity

invariants.statement: Aspect ratio, rendering quality, and typography remain identical across every segment of a production.

provenance.source: CFS-011 §3.1 Visual Identity

invariants.statement: Every segment shares the same protagonist, the same constitutional world, and one continuous timeline.

provenance.source: CFS-011 §3.2 Narrative Identity

invariants.statement: A recurring visual motif and a recurring constitutional symbol persist across every segment.

provenance.source: CFS-011 §3.2 Narrative Identity

invariants.statement: Every visual metaphor corresponds to one or more grounding invariants; no visual element implies a principle absent from the originating collection.

provenance.source: CFS-011 §3.3 Semantic Identity

invariants.statement: Each segment begins from the final semantic state of the preceding segment, preserving character position, environmental state, lighting direction, and active motifs.

provenance.source: CFS-011 §3.4 Transition Identity

invariants.statement: The production opens by establishing the subject's state before any constitutional change.

provenance.source: CFS-012 §5, beat 1/5

invariants.statement: An inciting realization introduces the constitutional principle the production will test.

provenance.source: CFS-012 §5, beat 2/5

invariants.statement: The subject moves through constitutional tension between the old order and the principle introduced.

provenance.source: CFS-012 §5, beat 3/5

invariants.statement: The tension resolves — the constitutional principle prevails, fails, or is qualified.

provenance.source: CFS-012 §5, beat 4/5

invariants.statement: The subject's constitutional transformation is shown as the production's final state.

provenance.source: CFS-012 §5, beat 5/5

invariants.statement: Every invariant class shall define its composition law before entering canonical status.

provenance.source: CFS-013 §3 — Invariant Composition Laws

invariants.statement: The runtime is closed; the ontology is open — new capability enters by ratifying a new invariant class, not by rewriting the engine.

provenance.source: CFS-013 §3 — the Open/Closed Principle applied constitutionally

invariants.statement: Every experience is the coherent composition of multiple invariant classes operating simultaneously within a shared constitutional context.

provenance.source: CFS-014 / Law XIV — Constitutional Coherence

invariants.statement: Knowledge composes locally; experiences succeed globally through coherence.

provenance.source: CFS-014 — the coherence field

invariants.statement: Every constitutional experience is the multiplicative composition of independently verifiable invariant fields; changing any field changes the whole experience.

provenance.source: Law XV — Compositional Fields (discovered by EXP-002's first production brief, 2026-07-04)

invariants.statement: Invariant fields are locally independent and globally dependent; a compositional failure can live purely in an interaction while every field is individually correct.

provenance.source: Law XV / CFS-013 §6 — evidenced by the terminal-beat defect caught by CFS-014

invariants.statement: A composed block that spans invariant families is scaffolding; it dissolves into its constituent classes as those classes are ratified, and no code may treat the mixed block as constitutionally final.

provenance.source: Law XV class-purity corollary — the v1 Continuity Block (CFS-011 backlog)

invariants.statement: Constitutional evolution occurs when the constitutional system detects a coherent pattern that no individual field reveals, and that pattern is subsequently ratified by human constitutional authority.

provenance.source: CFS-009 constitutional principle — Constitutional Evolution (first instance: Law XV, revealed by CFS-014, ratified 2026-07-04)

invariants.statement: Consequences are emergent properties of composition; they arise from interactions between fields, not from fields in isolation.

provenance.source: CFS-006a §7 — the compositional grounding of consequence engineering (type specimen: the terminal-beat defect)

invariants.statement: Consequence engineering is the discipline of composing fields whose interaction reliably produces desirable consequences — architecture, not forecasting.

provenance.source: CFS-006a §7 — restated under Law XV

invariants.statement: Constitutional fields shall compose according to a constitutionally valid sequence; correct components arranged in an invalid sequence do not constitute a coherent experience.

provenance.source: Law XV sequencing corollary — Constitutional Sequencing (CFS-013 §7, ratified 2026-07-05). Proposed as INV-075; issued as 078 — 075 was already taken and the append-only rule forbids renumbering. Type specimen: the EXP-002 terminal-beat defect re-read as a temporal-correctness failure.

invariants.statement: Local, relational, and temporal correctness are distinct properties: a field may be internally valid, agree with the others, and still occur out of constitutional order — each failure class requires its own validation.

provenance.source: CFS-013 §7 — the three-correctness taxonomy (local = field integrity, relational = composition, temporal = sequencing)

invariants.statement: Constitutional coherence is not merely the presence of correct components, but the emergence of a valid whole through correct composition and correct sequence.

provenance.source: CFS-009 constitutional principle — Constitutional Emergence (the bridge between Law XV and Law XIV, ratified 2026-07-05)

invariants.statement: An Invariant Field is a coherent domain of interacting invariants that governs the behaviour of a system; fields are independently verifiable, composable, and give rise to emergent phenomena through their interaction with other fields.

provenance.source: CFS-002 §2a — the universal Invariant Field definition, generalized from the constitutional-experience-scoped reading of Law XV after observing the same compositional mathematics governs biology, engineering, operating systems, civilization, and intelligence, not only constitutional experience (ratified 2026-07-06, operator + agent co-authored).

invariants.statement: Every invariant field shall be coherent (governs one concern), independent (verifiable in isolation), composable (contributes to system behaviour only through composition), versioned (evolves by supersession), and traceable (every observable behaviour can identify the fields that composed it).

provenance.source: CFS-002 §2a — the field properties.

invariants.statement: Every coherent system is the composition of independently verifiable invariant fields; constitutional experiences are one expression of this general principle.

provenance.source: Law XV, generalized 2026-07-06 (CFS-009 amendment). Broadens the SCOPE of inv.experience.072, not its content — 072 remains constitutionally recorded and true as the constitutional-scoped instance of this universal law.

invariants.statement: An Invariant Collection is a curated grouping of invariants; an Invariant Field is a functional role in composition that one or more collections occupy — a field is a role, not a container.

provenance.source: CFS-002 §2a — the Field/Collection/Namespace distinction, keeping storage, classification, and execution unconflated.

invariants.statement: Invariant fields may be natural (discovered — e.g. gravitation, genetics, thermodynamics) or constitutional (ratified — e.g. governance, protocols, experience architectures); both obey the same compositional mathematics, differing only in origin.

provenance.source: CFS-002 §2a — natural vs. constitutional invariant fields.

invariants.statement: The Semantic Field defines what may be constitutionally expressed — the principles, constraints, meanings, and invariants an experience draws on; without it there is no constitutional meaning.

provenance.source: CFS-002 §2a — the five constitutional fields (1/5).

invariants.statement: The Narrative Field defines when constitutional meaning is revealed — governing sequence, transformation, progression, and resolution; without it there is no constitutional journey.

provenance.source: CFS-002 §2a — the five constitutional fields (2/5).

invariants.statement: The Style Field defines how constitutional meaning is perceived — governing visual, linguistic, and aesthetic continuity; without it there is no constitutional identity.

provenance.source: CFS-002 §2a — the five constitutional fields (3/5).

invariants.statement: The Experience Field defines how constitutional meaning is encountered by a constitutional subject — governing personalization, agency, rendering, pacing, and orchestration; without it there is no constitutional interaction.

provenance.source: CFS-002 §2a — the five constitutional fields (4/5).

invariants.statement: The Context Field defines the circumstances under which every other field operates — context determines applicability, never truth; without it there is no constitutional judgment.

provenance.source: CFS-002 §2a — the five constitutional fields (5/5). Namespace remains unpopulated — Law XV's 'named slot' — pending ratified context invariants.

invariants.statement: A constitutional experience is an emergent property of field composition — it is not stored, it is composed.

provenance.source: CFS-002 §2a — Corollary III (Experience Emergence).

invariants.statement: Every constitutional experience shall be decomposable back into the fields and invariants from which it was composed.

provenance.source: CFS-002 §2a — Corollary IV (Constitutional Explainability).

invariants.statement: Temporal correctness is graded, not boolean: an experience's coherence varies with its composition order; the designed sequence is a coherence maximum, and alternative orderings may realise distinct coherent experiences at different coherence values.

provenance.source: CFS-013 §7 empirical refinement — EXP-002 sequencing control arm, 2026-07-05: reversed-order cut degraded coherence without zeroing it; operator insight that sequencing scores rather than passes/fails.

invariants.statement: Local coherence survives a global sequence violation: relational correctness is pairwise-local while temporal correctness is global, so adjacent units still agree even when the whole no longer unfolds in constitutional order.

provenance.source: CFS-013 §7 empirical refinement — EXP-002 sequencing control arm, 2026-07-05: the reversed cut's adjacent original pairs retained pairwise coherence while the global arc broke; the three-correctness taxonomy's rows are independently damageable and therefore independently validatable.

invariants.statement: Sequence is scored, not validated: temporal correctness is a graded coherence field over the space of orderings, with the designed sequence as a global maximum, possible local maxima at alternative coherent orderings, and graded decay away from the optimum.

provenance.source: CFS-013 §7 topological formalization (ratified 2026-07-05, operator + agent co-authored) — EXP-002 control arm evidence; supersedes the binary reading of the sequencing corollary's 'valid sequence', not the corollary itself.

invariants.statement: A remix does not change the work; it finds another coherent trajectory through the same invariant space — the invariants remain fixed, only the traversal changes.

provenance.source: CFS-013 §7 topological formalization — the constitutional ground for CFS-006's remix-with-lineage path: derivative works as alternative traversals of a fixed invariant substrate.

invariants.statement: Every constitutional cycle shall increase the capability of the Human Agency System: learning is remembering; improvement is becoming better.

provenance.source: CFS-015 principle 7 — Constitutional Improvement (amendment 2026-07-06, operator + co-agent review). Satisfied mechanically by the Constitutional Improvement Loop (Capability → Operation → Observation → Receipt → Learning → Improved Capability).

invariants.statement: The Constitutional Capability Pipeline produces capability, not code; development is one implementation mechanism among several (configuration, registry updates, prompts, policy, schemas, knowledge, automation, documentation) — capability remains the invariant.

provenance.source: CFS-015 Strand Two rename (amendment 2026-07-06) — the pipeline formerly called the Constitutional Development Pipeline; the Implementation Pack is the artifact produced immediately before the Implementation stage, not a stage itself.

invariants.statement: A constitutional system shall be built by the same constitutional principles it establishes; the program of its construction is its first constitutional capability.

provenance.source: CFS-015 preamble — the recursive implementation principle (amendment 2026-07-06): Chrysalis is not just building Constitutional Computing, it is demonstrating it.

invariants.statement: Platform sovereignty, like identity, is a bundle of capabilities: it is assessed dimension by dimension — model openness, provider choice, commercial independence, infrastructure — and never collapsed to a single boolean.

provenance.source: Operator direction 2026-07-06 (platform sovereignty is a bundle of capabilities; the Venice credit-wall case study) — CFS-018 Platform Sovereignty.

invariants.statement: Model sovereignty is the openness bundle: open weights, open scoring, and open access; each alone is insufficient.

provenance.source: Operator direction 2026-07-06 (platform sovereignty is a bundle of capabilities; the Venice credit-wall case study) — CFS-018 Platform Sovereignty.

invariants.statement: The operator shall be able to choose, switch, and combine model providers free of commercial or platform lock-in; provider choice is the essence of sovereignty, of which open weights are the maximum.

provenance.source: Operator direction 2026-07-06 (platform sovereignty is a bundle of capabilities; the Venice credit-wall case study) — CFS-018 Platform Sovereignty.

invariants.statement: No commercial gate — credits, billing, licensing, or account standing — shall be able to block constitutional operation; an open-weight model behind a credit wall is a sovereignty infringement.

provenance.source: Operator direction 2026-07-06 (platform sovereignty is a bundle of capabilities; the Venice credit-wall case study) — CFS-018 Platform Sovereignty.

invariants.statement: The operator shall hold agency over where the platform lives — hosting, storage, and execution, decentralized or centralized by the operator's choice — as a sovereignty dimension in its own right.

provenance.source: Operator direction 2026-07-06 (platform sovereignty is a bundle of capabilities; the Venice credit-wall case study) — CFS-018 Platform Sovereignty.

invariants.statement: Constitutional operation shall survive the loss of any single infrastructure provider, as it survives the loss of any single model provider.

provenance.source: Operator direction 2026-07-06 (platform sovereignty is a bundle of capabilities; the Venice credit-wall case study) — CFS-018 Platform Sovereignty.

invariants.statement: Every sovereignty dimension shall be measurable by experiment; an unmeasured sovereignty claim is a claim, not a capability.

provenance.source: Operator direction 2026-07-06 (platform sovereignty is a bundle of capabilities; the Venice credit-wall case study) — CFS-018 Platform Sovereignty.

invariants.statement: The Platform Sovereignty Experiment series measures the sovereignty bundle dimension by dimension; EXP-004 is its first member, measuring the model/provider dimension on the Sovereignty Scale.

provenance.source: Operator direction 2026-07-06 (platform sovereignty is a bundle of capabilities; the Venice credit-wall case study) — CFS-018 Platform Sovereignty.

invariants.statement: Constitutional Cybernetics is the study of constitutionally governed adaptive systems: how a system changes under constitutional feedback while remaining constitutional.

provenance.source: IRL PRD (operator, 2026-07-06) — CFS-019 Constitutional Cybernetics Research Laboratory charter.

invariants.statement: Operational evidence — receipts, experiment results, Reach — shall flow back into the constitutional substrate as candidate knowledge; the feedback loop is the institution.

provenance.source: IRL PRD (operator, 2026-07-06) — CFS-019 Constitutional Cybernetics Research Laboratory charter.

invariants.statement: A research institution shall operate by the principles it investigates; its own operation is its first and permanent experiment.

provenance.source: IRL PRD (operator, 2026-07-06) — CFS-019 Constitutional Cybernetics Research Laboratory charter.

invariants.statement: Constitutional adaptation never bypasses ratification: the system may propose its own evolution; the operator ratifies it.

provenance.source: IRL PRD (operator, 2026-07-06) — CFS-019 Constitutional Cybernetics Research Laboratory charter.

invariants.statement: Every conversation updates the constitutional state: generation is a state transition, never a terminal output.

provenance.source: Operator spec 2026-07-06 (DCIR — Dynamic Constitutional Interaction Runtime) — CFS-020.

invariants.statement: Every action emits observable events; an action without an event is invisible to the constitutional loop.

provenance.source: Operator spec 2026-07-06 (DCIR — Dynamic Constitutional Interaction Runtime) — CFS-020.

invariants.statement: Every artefact becomes part of the active reasoning context the moment it is generated; the system reasons over its own outputs.

provenance.source: Operator spec 2026-07-06 (DCIR — Dynamic Constitutional Interaction Runtime) — CFS-020.

invariants.statement: Every observation may refine future inference; a behavioural invariant discovered by observation is a proposed pattern, never a rule, and is never canonical without operator ratification.

provenance.source: Operator spec 2026-07-06 (DCIR — Dynamic Constitutional Interaction Runtime) — CFS-020.

invariants.statement: Every recommendation is contextual to the current constitutional state, never generated from conversation history alone.

provenance.source: Operator spec 2026-07-06 (DCIR — Dynamic Constitutional Interaction Runtime) — CFS-020.

invariants.statement: Interface affordances are generated from inferred intent within the operator's active context, never statically defined and never as orphan output.

provenance.source: Operator spec 2026-07-06 (DCIR — Dynamic Constitutional Interaction Runtime) — CFS-020.

invariants.statement: The cognitive-action loop remains continuous until the constitutional objective is satisfied; no single generation closes it.

provenance.source: Operator spec 2026-07-06 (DCIR — Dynamic Constitutional Interaction Runtime) — CFS-020.

invariants.statement: Knowledge is a computational object: its preservation, composition, and reasoning-compression are measurable properties.

provenance.source: Aletheon review (operator's co-agent), 2026-07-06 — CFS-019 institute-standing amendment.

invariants.statement: The platform is the instrument; the research programme is the enduring asset.

provenance.source: Aletheon review (operator's co-agent), 2026-07-06 — CFS-019 institute-standing amendment.

invariants.statement: Representation invariants preserve identity and connotation across media — what must always remain recognizable, as distinct from what must always remain true.

provenance.source: Operator articulation, 2026-07-07 — CFS-021 Constitutional Civic Futurism.

invariants.statement: A constitutional object is independent of its representation; representation is a projection of an invariant object into a modality.

provenance.source: Operator articulation, 2026-07-07 — CFS-021 Constitutional Civic Futurism.

invariants.statement: Every constitutional artifact has an invariant identity and one or more modality projections; navigation occurs through identity, not through representation.

provenance.source: Operator articulation, 2026-07-07 — CFS-021 Constitutional Civic Futurism.

invariants.statement: Every representation should function as an instrument — preserving identity, connotation, provenance, and navigability — existing not merely to communicate constitutional order but to enable people to reason within it.

provenance.source: Operator articulation, 2026-07-07 — CFS-021 Constitutional Civic Futurism.

invariants.statement: Operational representation invariants are representational structures whose purpose is to enable reasoning, navigation, and interaction with the constitutional field, not merely to preserve identity and connotation.

provenance.source: Operator articulation, 2026-07-07 — CFS-021 Constitutional Civic Futurism.

invariants.statement: There are three canonical families of invariants: natural invariants are discovered, constitutional invariants are ratified, and representation invariants are composed.

provenance.source: Operator articulation, 2026-07-07 — CFS-021 Constitutional Civic Futurism.

invariants.statement: Civilizational evolution proceeds by discovering new classes of invariants and reorganizing existing systems around them; metamorphosis reconstitutes an existing world around a deeper invariant order rather than building a new world from new parts.

provenance.source: Operator articulation, 2026-07-07 — CFS-021 Constitutional Civic Futurism (the Chrysalis theory; hypothesis-standing).

invariants.statement: A style is an interpretation of the representation invariants, as a modality is a projection of them; identity and connotation are preserved across interpretations as across modalities. The system is the invariants; a style is one interpretation the system accommodates, never its definition. An interpretation is valid only if it satisfies the invariant contract.

provenance.source: Operator refinement, 2026-07-07 — CFS-021: a cohesive system that accommodates many interpretations, not a single style.

invariants.statement: Surface material — translucency, blur, elevation — is a representation role: an interpretation binds material as it binds colour, and 'flat' (opaque, no blur, no shadow) is a valid material. Colour alone cannot express a rendering system.

provenance.source: Operator articulation, 2026-07-08 — CFS-021: the AgentiQ Liquid Glass interpretation (the platform house style) required the representation contract to carry a MATERIAL role family alongside colour/type/motion.

invariants.statement: Personhood shall remain continuously individualizable without identity exposure: a constitutional subject persists across time, interactions, and substrates — provably the same subject through personhood proof, a sovereign continuity root, and accrued standing — while identity disclosure remains a discrete, consent-gated act that continuity never requires.

provenance.source: CFS-009 Law XIII — Individualization (binding law); operator articulation 2026-07-14; implementation witness IRL-010 §2.11

invariants.statement: Experimental instruments must faithfully report observed outcomes regardless of whether those outcomes support the hypothesis. An instrument that can only report success is not an instrument.

provenance.source: IRL Principle 004 — operator-ratified 2026-07-14. Elevated from the EXP-003 breadth-arm instrument-honesty fix (a hardcoded-optimistic summary that misreported a negative result). Normative constitutional commitment: the runtime is constitutionally bound to truthfully represent evidence.

invariants.statement: Knowledge quality scales through organization before it scales through accumulation: a knowledge substrate improves not because it grows, but because experience reorganizes it. Value is set by constitutional organization and retrieval, not by quantity contained.

provenance.source: Operator-ratified as governing principle 2026-07-14 (advisor: the institute's first emerging scientific law). EMPIRICAL confirmation PENDING EXP-003 Run 002 (Reasoning Economics — standing-weighted vs confidence-weighted retrieval); ratified as research posture, not yet document-verified. First supporting datum: EXP-003 Run 001 (curation dominates accumulation).

invariants.statement: The value of an invariant lies not in how it was discovered, but in its stability across contexts and its ability to organize future reasoning: reasoning that begins from a ratified, reused invariant is not a shortcut relative to reasoning that regenerates it from scratch — invariance, not origin, is the criterion of epistemic worth.

provenance.source: IRL Principle 005 — operator-ratified 2026-07-15 in dialogue with Alethean: "the origin of the invariance is not what is important, it is the invariance of the invariance." Alethean's rephrasing ratified as the canonical statement. Answers the critique (attributed to Austin's Claude) that invariant-extraction work differs from prompt engineering or RAG because it commits to a validated, reusable claim rather than retrieving context. Distinct from 131 (instrument honesty) and 132 (organization scales knowledge quality); related to but not a restatement of either.

invariants.statement: Discovery importance: an explicitly published, deployed, or resolved experience is more consequential to surface than unpublished or unresolved catalogue content — the operator's own act of publishing is a constitutional signal of importance.

provenance.source: CFS-035 §10 — the discovery-ranking node's IMPORTANCE dimension, extracted from the scoreCapsule +10 (sourceType==='experience') and +6 (assetStatus==='resolved') magic numbers. The governing invariant whose earned standing weights this dimension (deriveDimensionWeights in services/invariants/nodes/discoveryRanking.ts). Operator-ratified 2026-07-18 with a seed validation prior of 4 (→ standing ≈44.4), encoding its ratified priority among the four discovery dimensions (need>importance>trust>novelty); the prior is superseded by earned validation as the shadow loop accrues real evidence.

invariants.statement: Discovery novelty: content that is new to this operator should be surfaced above content they have already seen or selected — discovery must widen, not merely re-present. Realising this dimension requires per-operator exposure/selection history from the Evolution face (a documented Phase-0 gap, currently neutral).

provenance.source: CFS-035 §10 — the discovery-ranking node's NOVELTY dimension. Phase 0 neutral (no incumbent magic number; scoreCapsule has no novelty term). Names the constitutional intent so the gap is explicit rather than silently absent. Operator-ratified 2026-07-18 with a seed validation prior of 2 (→ standing ≈28.6) — lowest of the four because its runtime signal (operator exposure history) is not yet wired, so its weight is presently inert (the dimension value is 0); the principle is nonetheless ratified and earns weight once the signal lands.

invariants.statement: Discovery trust: content of published or curated provenance (smart-content, showcase-focus codexes, recognised catalogue tokens) carries more authority than generic catalogue content and should rank accordingly — provenance is a projection of trust.

provenance.source: CFS-035 §10 — the discovery-ranking node's TRUST dimension, extracted from the scoreCapsule +4 (smart-content), +3 (showcase codexSlug) and +2 (showcase token match) magic numbers. Operator-ratified 2026-07-18 with a seed validation prior of 3 (→ standing ≈37.5), ranked below importance and need in the discovery priority order; superseded by earned validation.

invariants.statement: Discovery need: content that serves the operator's active intent (modality/kind fit and prompt-term overlap) should rank above content that does not — discovery is a projection of what the operator is asking for right now, not a static popularity order.

provenance.source: CFS-035 §10 — the discovery-ranking node's NEED dimension, extracted from the scoreCapsule intent↔modality/kind fit branches (+4/+3/+8/+5/+2/+3) and the +1-per-matching-term prompt overlap loop. Operator-ratified 2026-07-18 with a seed validation prior of 5 (→ standing =50.0), the HIGHEST of the four — serving the operator's active intent is the paramount discovery law. Superseded by earned validation as the shadow loop accrues real evidence.

invariants.statement: The Pre-Prompt Reasoning Theorem: prompt engineering optimizes how reasoning is requested; context engineering optimizes what knowledge is supplied; invariant intelligence optimizes which reasoning need not occur again. These are three distinct pre-inference compressions with three distinct optimization targets, not three techniques within one discipline.

provenance.source: CRP-002 §0.2 (Three Computational Compressions amendment, 2026-07-18) — the Pre-Prompt Reasoning Theorem, operator-formulated in the dialogue with Austin and Austin's agent. Ratified as the field's organizing theorem; the optimization claim (that computational reuse is a distinct and superior objective for a class of intents) is the falsifiable hypothesis the Institute exists to test, hence validated rather than canonical.

invariants.statement: A structural invariant is a persistent representation of previously validated reasoning that remains sufficient across a class of intents and can therefore be reused rather than recomputed. It is not a fact, not knowledge, and not truth — it is compressed, reusable reasoning.

provenance.source: CRP-002 §0.4 (Three Computational Compressions amendment, 2026-07-18) — the tightened definition of a structural invariant, operator-ratified. Sharpens inv.reasoning.081/083 (the Invariant Field) and the CRP-002 invariant-as-projection reading by naming what an invariant IS: previously validated reasoning, not a fact/knowledge/truth. Definitional canon.

invariants.statement: The Three Computational Compressions: every intelligent system performs a compression before inference, and the discriminating question is what it compresses. Prompt engineering compresses instructions (output: a prompt); context engineering compresses knowledge (output: curated knowledge); invariant intelligence compresses reasoning (output: an invariant substrate). Because the optimization target differs, invariant intelligence is a distinct category, not a better form of context.

provenance.source: CRP-002 §0.1 (Three Computational Compressions amendment, 2026-07-18) — the Three Computational Compressions taxonomy, operator-formulated (elevated from three engineering techniques to three computational compressions, tying into the Institute's compression theory, CFS-008). Definitional canon.

invariants.statement: The purpose of invariant intelligence is not to retrieve better knowledge; it is to reduce the amount of reasoning that must be performed at inference time by reusing previously validated structural reasoning. The distinguishing optimization objective is computational reuse, not information relevance — representation and runtime exist to support this proposition, not to constitute it.

provenance.source: CRP-002 §0.3 (Three Computational Compressions amendment, 2026-07-18) — the canonical purpose statement and the identification of the independent variable (computational reuse), operator-formulated in the Austin dialogue. A precise, experimentally-falsifiable scientific claim; ratified as the programme's central claim, empirical confirmation via the EXP-nnn lifecycle — hence validated.

invariants.statement: The Representation Principle: representations are not invariants but manifestations of invariants. The same structural invariant may be represented as natural language, symbolic logic, a graph, executable code, or a future world-model representation. The representation is constrained by the computational substrate; the invariant is not. Two pre-prompt processes that serialize to similar text need not have performed the same computation — one may have rediscovered the relationships from raw knowledge, the other assembled previously validated invariants.

provenance.source: CRP-002 §0.5 (Three Computational Compressions amendment, 2026-07-18) — the Representation Principle, operator-ratified. Answers the conflation (attributed to Austin's agent) that because everything serializes to tokens the computation is identical: the representation is the transport, not the invariant. Reconciles with and sharpens the CRP-002 2026-07-16 Representation Gauntlet amendment (representation as experimental handle vs the substrate it manifests). Separates structural invariance, constitutional governance, and representation into three distinct concerns. Definitional canon.

invariants.statement: The Structural–Constitutional Convergence Principle: structural invariants establish the persistent substrate of intelligence independently of any particular implementation or system; constitutional invariants provide the mechanisms by which intelligent systems operationalize, interpret, govern, and evolve their reliance upon that substrate. Constitutional mechanisms therefore do not modify structural truth; they regulate the justified operational use of structural truth within an intelligent system. The two classes meet not in the invariant itself but in the interface — the operationalization of truth.

provenance.source: CRP-002 §0.7 (2026-07-18) — the Structural–Constitutional Convergence Principle, operator-ratified. The coupling mechanism between the science (structural invariants: what is true) and the governance (constitutional invariants: how a system operationalizes that truth). Explains why the runtime (CFS-035) belongs to the second half of the architecture. Extends the structural/constitutional separation (Convergence Log Entry 004) by naming the interface at which they meet. Definitional canon.

invariants.statement: Structural invariants are anchored in reality: a structural invariant is a real regularity of the world (gravity, the Pythagorean relation) that holds independently of any observer, computation, or representation. An intelligent system — natural or synthetic — most often reasons about a structural invariant not directly but through a representation (physics, mathematics, code): the representation is the proxy interface, the invariant is the reality it is anchored in. Constitutional invariants, by contrast, are properties of the systems that reason about reality — they govern justified reliance, not truth. The first is the science of Invariant Intelligence; the second is the engineering of Constitutional Computing; where they meet is the operationalization of truth.

provenance.source: CRP-002 §0.7 (2026-07-18) — operator-ratified. The clean object-of-discovery vs mechanism-of-use separation, un-hedged after the Representation stratum (§0.8) made it safe to say the invariant IS the real regularity (gravity example): humans experience gravity directly and reason about it through representations; the invariant is anchored in reality. Reconciled with inv.epistemology.139 via the access-and-codification bridge (inv.epistemology.150): the invariant IN REALITY is not a representation, but a system accesses and codifies it only AS a representation, so 139 (the invariant as held) and 144 (the invariant in reality) are two aspects of one invariant, not a contradiction. Two things, not three: the real invariant and its representation (which is both the held form and the interface). Consistent with inv.reasoning.085 / inv.constitutional.126. Definitional canon.

invariants.statement: Standing is operational confidence, not truth: it measures the degree to which a system is justified in operationalizing an invariant within a given constitutional context — it separates Use from Truth. Truth belongs to the structural layer; standing answers 'given what we currently know, how confidently should this system rely on this invariant right now?' — a governance question, not a scientific one.

provenance.source: CRP-002 §0.6 (2026-07-18) — operator-ratified. Sharpens inv.constitutional.061 (standing = constitutional confidence, never a measure of truth) by naming the Use axis explicitly: standing measures justified OPERATIONALIZATION, not truth. Reaffirms inv.constitutional.018/060/062 (evidence not popularity; validation within a domain of applicability; Reach≠validity — Law XII). Does not supersede 061; extends it.

invariants.statement: The invariant field model: intent activates the invariant field (it determines direction — which field becomes active); structural invariants define the field's topology; standing shapes the operational potential landscape within it; projection follows the gradient to the governing invariants. Standing is intent-relative — an invariant's standing is evaluated only against the class of intents it governs, so an obscure invariant rarely invoked may hold extremely high standing (strong 'invariant mass') if it consistently proves structurally sufficient whenever its governing intent arises.

provenance.source: CRP-002 §0.6 (2026-07-18) — operator-ratified. Formalizes the field/potential-landscape picture (intent = direction, standing = depth/operational potential, projection = following the gradient) and canonizes intent-relative standing. Composes the CRP-002 intent-as-projection operator with the Law XII standing canon (inv.constitutional.060/061/062). 'Invariant mass': importance is attractor-strength under the governing intent, not invocation frequency.

invariants.statement: Invariant Intelligence has three strata by role: Structural invariants (ontology — what is fundamentally true; properties of reality, independent of observer, computation, and representation); Invariant Representations (interface — how a structural invariant becomes computationally accessible: language, mathematics, symbolic logic, code, graphs, images, DNA, world models, and forms not yet invented); and Constitutional invariants (governance — how a system justifies, trusts, governs, and operationalizes that truth). Representation is not a type of invariant; it is the interface through which invariants become accessible. A structural invariant becomes usable by an intelligent system only through a representation, and the representation is never the invariant.

provenance.source: CRP-002 §0.8 (2026-07-18) — operator-ratified. The missing third pillar: Invariant Representations as the INTERFACE stratum between structural invariants (the science) and the constitutional runtime (the governance). Corrects the co-equal-'type' grouping (inv.constitutional.126 'representation invariants are composed') — the inv.representation.* family (CFS-021, 121-124) are constitutional-in-function invariants that GOVERN the interface; the interface stratum itself is not a type of structural truth. Reconciled against the CFS-019 axis-precision rule: these strata are a ROLE decomposition (ontology/interface/governance), NOT to be equated with CFS-019's Layer I/II/III governance-axis, the structural/constitutional function axis, or inv.reasoning.085's natural/constitutional origin axis. Extends inv.reasoning.142 (representations are manifestations, not invariants) and inv.representation.122 (a constitutional object is independent of its representation).

invariants.statement: The architecture-limitation principle: the inability of a particular computational architecture to faithfully represent or reason over an invariant does not diminish the existence or validity of the invariant — it reveals a limitation of the architecture, not of the invariant. Alphanumeric text is today's dominant representation, not the substrate; that an LLM runtime cannot collapse a representation into alphanumerics does not stop the invariant being true.

provenance.source: CRP-002 §0.8 (2026-07-18) — operator-ratified. Generalizes the representation-independence claim beyond LLMs: explains LLM spatial-reasoning gaps, human high-dimensional-mathematics limits, and the emergence of world models as architecture limits, never invariant limits. Answers Austin's agent's 'everything reaches the LLM as tokens' — true of the implementation, not the theory. Sharpens inv.reasoning.142 ('the representation is constrained by the computational substrate; the invariant is not') by naming the architecture's reasoning capability, not only its representational form.

invariants.statement: Operational truth vs absolute truth: the three strata (structural, representation, constitutional) are themselves invariant FIELDS, and in an absolute sense these fields overlap at their boundaries — where one ends and another begins is a matter of magnification and perspective. The Institute's separations are OPERATIONAL truths: sufficient and sharp at the working magnification, not claims of absolute, non-overlapping boundaries. The definitions hold operationally, which is what the discipline requires; absolute cleanliness is neither claimed nor needed.

provenance.source: CRP-002 §0.8 (2026-07-18) — operator-ratified. The epistemic-register caveat on the whole architecture: the strata (and invariant fields generally, inv.reasoning.081/082) are operationally separable, not absolutely disjoint — the fields overlap by magnification/perspective. Names the OPERATIONAL-vs-ABSOLUTE truth register the discipline works in — the same register standing operates in (inv.constitutional.145, standing = operational confidence). Lineage (operator-attributed): this register is the Polity Papers' FACT — a 'Fundamentally Accurate Current Truth' (fundamentally accurate + current/operational, not absolute). The Polity Papers are DVN-receipted in the Qriptopian Cartridge but not yet ingested into this register; canonizing their FACT corpus here is tracked backlog. Contextualizes 082's 'independent (verifiable in isolation)' as an operational abstraction, not an absolute-disjointness claim. Protects the doctrine against a false precision the clean diagrams could imply.

invariants.statement: Access and codification: a structural invariant is real (anchored in reality), but an intelligent system accesses and codifies it only as a representation — a system never holds the raw regularity, only a representation of it. Therefore a structural invariant AS HELD BY A SYSTEM is a persistent representation of previously validated reasoning (inv.epistemology.139) about a regularity that IN ITSELF is real (inv.epistemology.144). The two definitions are not in tension: 139 describes the invariant as accessed and codified within a system; 144 describes the invariant in reality. Gravity is real; a system reaches it only through representations of it.

provenance.source: CRP-002 §0.4/§0.7 (2026-07-18) — operator-ratified. The bridge that reconciles inv.epistemology.139 (a structural invariant is a persistent representation of previously validated reasoning) with inv.epistemology.144 (a structural invariant is a real regularity anchored in reality). Operator: '139 technically is true — the clarification is that the structural invariant is accessed and codified as a representation. Gravity is real but accessed via a system through representations of it.' Collapses the earlier mis-framing of three objects (real regularity / held form / interface) into two: the real invariant, and its representation (which serves as both the codified held form and the interface, inv.reasoning.147). Composes with inv.reasoning.142 (representations are manifestations of invariants) and inv.epistemology.148 (architecture limits are not invariant limits).
